hq: 2261 Market Street STE 22560, San Francisco, CA 94114
signing_authority: Nguyen Anh Mac, CEO of Truth Systems, September 19, 2025

system_description.overview: Truth Systems is an AI-powered governance and compliance platform for law firms. It monitors interactions with AI software to ensure they are safe and compliant with internal firm policy, client guidelines, and AI-usage best practices. The platform leverages a browser extension and AI to warn users when their behavior violates policies.

products.Automated Governance and Compliance Mana: End-to-end governance and compliance monitoring and guardrails for client guidelines and firm policies; browser extension-based monitoring capabilities within client web environments.
products.Compliance Support Services: White-glove onboarding and implementation support; access to dedicated governance expertise and legal AI specialists; complementary workshops for executive leadership on AI governance domains.

regions: Not specifically named; single region inferred from diagram
infrastructure.physical_presence: No physical offices; no physical servers — all cloud
infrastructure.availability_zones: Multi-AZ (multiple availability zones explicitly mentioned for DR failover)
additional_cloud: Azure (client tenancy for Entra ID — client-side only)

network_architecture.network_segmentation: Segmented VPCs with ACLs; customer accounts are segmented from each other (single-tenancy model — multiple production AWS accounts per client)
network_architecture.api_gateway: AWS App Runner (acts as application gateway)
network_architecture.firewalls: System firewalls on application gateway and production network; firewall rules reviewed annually by IT management
network_architecture.ids_ips: AWS IDS and IPS deployed to identify and protect against threats
network_architecture.vpn: VPN Gateway; remote access via bastion host with VPN
network_architecture.tls: TLS via third-party certificate authority for all web-based system access
network_architecture.content_filtering: Content filtering system routed through proxy server; all systems restricted to internet via proxy
network_architecture.topology: Users -> Route 53 -> Internet Gateway -> VPC (App Runner -> AWS RDS; VPC Endpoint -> AWS AI Services); VPN Gateway -> Internet (via VPN) -> Client Azure Tenancy (Entra ID). Single-tenancy: separate AWS production accounts per client.

application_architecture.environments: Separate development/test and production environments; changes tested before deployment to Stage or Production

data_storage.backup_frequency: Daily (backups taken daily per CC7.4.2 test evidence)

authentication_access_control.identity_provider: Azure Entra ID (client-side for end-user auth); AWS IAM (internal infrastructure)
authentication_access_control.mfa: MFA enforced for all administrative access to production platform; MFA required for source code repository access; 2FA for employee email
authentication_access_control.password_policy: Periodic forced changes, expiry, complexity requirements via AWS password policy; accounts disabled after limited failed logon attempts; no guest/anonymous logins

encryption.at_rest: Cloud provider key management service (AWS KMS implied)
encryption.key_management: AWS KMS (cloud provider key management service); access to keys restricted to authorized individuals

ci_cd_devops.source_control: Not named (generic 'source code management tool' with MFA-gated access)
ci_cd_devops.branch_protection: Code repository branch rules configured; every merge request to production requires additional approval; developers cannot push to production without approval
ci_cd_devops.iac: Configuration management tool used for baseline configurations and rollback (specific tool not named)
ci_cd_devops.patch_management: Automatic patch management set up by CTO; monthly patch management reviews via IT Leadership Committee; workstations scanned daily for patch compliance; critical OS patches applied as available

monitoring_logging.apm_tool: Not named (internal monitoring tool with threshold-based alerting)
monitoring_logging.log_aggregation: Application and infrastructure level logging (specific tool not named)
monitoring_logging.alerting: Automated alerts for high and critical risk events; tracked in change-management/ticketing application

third_party_services.purpose: Cloud infrastructure, compute (App Runner), database (RDS), networking (VPC, Route 53), AI services, IDS/IPS, KMS
Client-side identity provider for end-user authentication
AI model inference (shown in architecture diagram)

security_tools.antivirus: Endpoint security solution with continuous automatic scanning; anti-virus on all desktops/laptops; definitions updated daily; deployed on Mac workstations and Windows systems
security_tools.vulnerability_scanning: Monthly automated vulnerability scans on infrastructure and applications; self-assessment scan prior to every production release
security_tools.penetration_testing: Annual external penetration testing by independent third party

untestable: CC2.2.3 — No significant personnel changes during engagement period (could not test internal communication of role changes)
CC3.2.1 — Cybersecurity insurance incidents: no cyber incidents occurred during engagement period
CC6.5.2 — Customer data deletion upon termination: no customer terminations during engagement period
CC7.3.1 — Security incident response: no security incidents reported during engagement period
excluded_criteria: CC6.4 — Physical access to data center facilities: AWS is responsible for physical security
A1.2 — Environmental protections: Truth Systems has no office/physical servers; managed by building management/AWS
notable_controls: Single-tenancy model: separate AWS production accounts per client — unusually strong tenant isolation
Client Azure Entra ID integration for end-user identity
IT Leadership Committee (ITLC) meets weekly to review security and controls
Risk and Governance Executive Committee (RGEC) empowered by Board for security oversight
BCDR tested annually across multiple loss scenarios

infrastructure.rationale: AWS single-region with multi-AZ, App Runner, RDS, VPN, IDS/IPS. Strong tenant isolation via separate AWS accounts per client. No multi-region. No CDN. No named IaC tool.

application_architecture.rationale: Browser extension + cloud backend pattern is unique to the use case. Branch protection and code review enforced. No languages/frameworks disclosed. Separate environments confirmed.

data_layer.rationale: AWS RDS confirmed, daily backups, multi-AZ, data classification policy, per-tenant segregation. No caching layer, warehouse, or file storage disclosed.

security_posture.rationale: IDS/IPS (AWS), firewalls, MFA, annual external pen test, monthly vuln scans, endpoint protection, cybersecurity insurance, content filtering/proxy. No WAF named. No SIEM named.

devops_maturity.rationale: Branch protection, code review, separate environments, emergency change process, automatic patch management. CI/CD tool and IaC tool not named. No container orchestration.

bcdr_readiness.rationale: BCDR policy documented and reviewed annually, tested annually across multiple scenarios, daily backups, multi-AZ failover. No RTO/RPO stated. No multi-region failover.

vendor_diversity.rationale: Heavy AWS concentration for all infrastructure. Azure Entra ID used on client side. AWS AI Services for inference. Limited disclosed vendor diversity; single cloud provider dependency.

overall.rationale: Truth Systems demonstrates a clean Type 2 with zero exceptions. Single-tenancy AWS model with strong per-client isolation is a genuine differentiator. Core gaps: RTO/RPO not disclosed, no WAF, SIEM or named IaC tool, heavy AWS dependency, no multi-region.

red_flags: Heavy AWS single-vendor concentration for all infrastructure
yellow_flags: CI/CD tool name not disclosed — hard to assess pipeline maturity
IaC tool not named — configuration management maturity unclear
No SIEM named — log aggregation and threat detection tooling opaque
No multi-region failover — single region failure would be a full outage
No CDN — could affect latency and DDoS resilience
green_flags: Zero exceptions across all 58 controls tested — clean Type 2 opinion
Single-tenancy model with separate AWS accounts per client — unusually strong tenant isolation for a startup
Annual external penetration testing by independent third party
MFA enforced on all administrative and source code access
Quarterly access reviews confirmed as operating
Access revocation within 1 business day — confirmed by auditor testing
BCDR tested annually with multiple loss scenarios
IT Leadership Committee meets weekly for security review
Client Azure Entra ID integration — customers retain identity control
key_observations: The 'Charter's Cloud Architecture' diagram label (vs Truth Systems branding) suggests this report template or diagram was adapted from another engagement — minor but worth noting
Single-tenancy via separate AWS accounts is expensive and operationally complex but represents a strong security commitment unusual at this company stage
Azure Entra ID appears only on the client side — this means Truth Systems integrates into law firms' existing SSO/directory, which reduces credential sprawl risk for clients
AWS AI Services in the architecture suggests use of managed ML services (e.g., Amazon Bedrock or SageMaker) rather than self-hosted models
The scope explicitly excludes Processing Integrity and Privacy trust criteria — relevant for a platform handling law firm client data
3-month audit period (Jun–Sep 2025) is the minimum for Type 2; shorter periods warrant monitoring for renewal
